Title: Regulation of Specialized Metabolism by WRKY Transcription Factors

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY TF family; WRKYGQK motif; C2H2/C2HC zinc finger
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtWRKY12 is reported to regulate lignin biosynthesis. 2. It is listed as a WRKY controlling specialized metabolism in the phenylpropanoid pathway. 3. It is discussed as an ortholog of Medicago STP with conserved metabolic function across species.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved WRKY regulator associated with lignin pathway control across species.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant rigidity; defense against microbes; deterrence of herbivory
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article; details summarized from Wang et al. (2010).
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modify lignification and related phenylpropanoid traits.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY TF family; WRKYGQK motif; C2H2/C2HC zinc finger
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates flavonol production in an auxin-inducible manner
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtWRKY23 regulates flavonol production. 2. Its action is auxin inducible. 3. The flavonols produced can negatively feed back on auxin signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as an auxin-responsive WRKY connecting hormone signaling to flavonol accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: polar auxin transport inhibition; UV protection; herbivore defense
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: negative feedback on auxin signaling via flavonol production
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also linked to nematode resistance in cited work.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering flavonol-associated stress and developmental traits.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates camalexin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY33 is a key regulator of camalexin biosynthesis. 2. MPK3/MPK6 directly phosphorylates WRKY33. 3. Activated WRKY33 induces PHYTOALEXIN DEFICIENT3, a key camalexin biosynthetic gene. 4. In the MPK4 pathway, WRKY33 is released from a VQ protein complex after phosphorylation-dependent destabilization.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key MAPK-responsive activator linking immune signaling to camalexin production.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior phosphorylation and target-gene activation studies; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MPK3/MPK6 cascade; MPK4 pathway; pathogen signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts within MPK4–VQ MOTIF-CONTAINING PROTEIN21 complex; phosphorylation destabilizes complex and releases WRKY33
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MPK3; MPK6; MPK4; VQ MOTIF-CONTAINING PROTEIN21
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for engineering phytoalexin-mediated disease resistance.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates proanthocyanin production and pectinaceous seed mucilage
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtWRKY44/TTG2 was the first WRKY characterized in specialized metabolism. 2. It regulates proanthocyanin biosynthesis. 3. It also regulates pectinaceous seed mucilage production. 4. Its expression is governed by a MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex connected to anthocyanin regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important node integrating WRKY regulation with the MYB-bHLH-WD40 metabolic network.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Expression governed by MYB-bHLH-WD40 TF complex
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for modifying seed coat tannins and mucilage traits.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively regulates artemisinin biosynthesis; overexpression doubled artemisinin yield in a high-yielding cultivar
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaWRKY1 regulates artemisinin biosynthesis in Artemisia annua. 2. Overexpression doubled artemisinin yield in a high-yielding cultivar. 3. Despite higher artemisinin, only one pathway gene was significantly up-regulated, indicating relief of a pathway bottleneck. 4. The study provides strong evidence that WRKYs can be used to engineer specialized metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Controls a critical bottleneck in the artemisinin pathway and has direct metabolic engineering value.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in high-yielding cultivar; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: one artemisinin pathway gene significantly up-regulated (not specified in this review text)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for overexpression or stacking to improve artemisinin yield.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: terpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis and pathway flux control
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates TIA biosynthesis and alters catharanthine and serpentine accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrWRKY1 directly regulates TRYPTOPHAN DECARBOXYLASE, which forms the indolic precursor tryptamine. 2. RNAi hairy root lines show altered accumulation of catharanthine and serpentine, indicating pathway flux control. 3. W-boxes are common in TIA promoters, supporting a broad regulatory role. 4. The promoter of CrWRKY1 contains cis-elements for bHLH, DOF, MYB, and TGACG-binding factors, suggesting regulation by higher-order TFs.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Directly controls precursor formation and branch flux in the TIA pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA interference in hairy roots; promoter analysis; direct target regulation evidence
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TRYPTOPHAN DECARBOXYLASE (TDC); several key TFs involved in TIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates TDC; regulates key TIA TFs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: jasmonate-responsive context; possible bHLH, DOF, MYB, TGACG-binding TF regulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for reprogramming TIA precursor supply and pathway flux.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: dictates expression of nine genes involved in berberine biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CjWRKY1 dictates the expression of nine genes involved in berberine biosynthesis. 2. It appears to up-regulate an entire specialized metabolic pathway. 3. It does not appear to affect primary metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pathway-wide activator of berberine biosynthesis with specificity for specialized metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: nine genes involved in berberine biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent candidate for pathway-level activation in BIA engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: heterologous expression affects accumulation of sanguinarine and chelirubine
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis AtWRKY1 heterologously expressed in California poppy altered sanguinarine and chelirubine accumulation. 2. This demonstrates that WRKY genes with known metabolic roles can be used across species. 3. It supports engineering of species with limited pathway information using heterologous WRKY TFs.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Demonstrates cross-species utility of WRKY regulators for alkaloid engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports heterologous TF engineering in medicinal plants with poorly characterized pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species differs from applied species; this distinction is preserved.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: binds TYROSINE DECARBOXYLASE promoter and possibly regulates BIA production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsWRKY1 binds the TYROSINE DECARBOXYLASE promoter. 2. It possibly regulates benzylisoquinoline alkaloid production. 3. W-boxes were found in seven available BIA gene promoters, suggesting broader WRKY involvement.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Promoter-binding evidence links this WRKY to upstream regulation of BIA precursor supply.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding assay; promoter motif analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for BIA pathway engineering, though functional effect needs fuller validation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates a sesquiterpene cyclase at a branch point leading to gossypol production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GaWRKY1 regulates a sesquiterpene cyclase. 2. The target step is at a pathway branch point leading to gossypol synthesis. 3. This established an early role of WRKY TFs in terpene specialized metabolism outside Arabidopsis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Controls branch-point flux toward gossypol through regulation of sesquiterpene cyclase expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifeedant phytoalexin; herbivore and pathogen defense
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for adjusting cotton defense metabolite content.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: expression is associated with increased rubber biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HbWRKY41 expression is associated with increased natural rubber biosynthesis. 2. It is discussed as part of rubber-related WRKY regulators in Hevea. 3. Orthologs of HbWRKY41 regulate phenylpropanoid metabolism in other species.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Association evidence implicates HbWRKY41 as an important rubber-related WRKY regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression-metabolite association
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker/regulator for improving rubber yield in long-lived trees.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic target genes not detailed in this review excerpt.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: expression is associated with increased rubber biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HbWRKY1 expression correlates with increased rubber biosynthesis. 2. It is part of a set of Hevea WRKYs implicated in rubber metabolism. 3. Its ortholog AtWRKY1 affects alkaloid accumulation in California poppy, suggesting conserved or repurposed function.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Expression correlation supports involvement in rubber metabolic regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression-metabolite association
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for rubber yield improvement and cross-species WRKY discovery.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates expression of SMALL RUBBER PARTICLE PROTEIN involved in rubber storage
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HbWRKY33 was identified in the Hevea WRKY family as regulating SMALL RUBBER PARTICLE PROTEIN expression. 2. This connects WRKY regulation to rubber storage/accumulation machinery. 3. HbWRKY33 is orthologous to terpene/lignin-related WRKYs in other species.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Controls expression of a key rubber particle-associated protein required for rubber storage.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Family characterization and target-gene expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SMALL RUBBER PARTICLE PROTEIN expression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance rubber particle formation and storage traits.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates expression of SMALL RUBBER PARTICLE PROTEIN involved in rubber storage
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HbWRKY14 regulates expression of SMALL RUBBER PARTICLE PROTEIN. 2. This links WRKY activity to rubber storage processes in Hevea.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the WRKY set controlling a key rubber particle protein.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Family characterization and target-gene expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SMALL RUBBER PARTICLE PROTEIN expression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for rubber yield improvement.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates expression of SMALL RUBBER PARTICLE PROTEIN involved in rubber storage
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HbWRKY55 regulates expression of SMALL RUBBER PARTICLE PROTEIN. 2. This supports WRKY-mediated control of rubber storage machinery in Hevea.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to regulation of a critical rubber particle-associated protein.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Family characterization and target-gene expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SMALL RUBBER PARTICLE PROTEIN expression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for rubber improvement.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression in tobacco alters accumulation of rutin, kaempferol, caffeic acid, and lignin
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtWRKY100577 was individually overexpressed in tobacco. 2. It altered accumulation of several phenylpropanoid metabolites, including rutin, kaempferol, caffeic acid, and lignin. 3. This illustrates that multiple WRKY TFs can affect the same metabolites.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Demonstrates transferable phenylpropanoid regulatory capacity in heterologous systems.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression in tobacco; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species differs from applied species.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression in tobacco alters accumulation of rutin, kaempferol, caffeic acid, and lignin
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtWRKY100630 was individually overexpressed in tobacco. 2. It altered several phenylpropanoid metabolites and lignin. 3. This supports overlap among WRKY regulators of the same metabolic outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Shows heterologous regulation of multiple phenylpropanoid outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression in tobacco; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species differs from applied species.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression in tobacco alters accumulation of rutin, kaempferol, caffeic acid, and lignin
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtWRKY108715 altered phenylpropanoid metabolite accumulation when overexpressed in tobacco. 2. It is also discussed as part of a conserved ortholog group independently linked to specialized metabolism across species.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Member of a conserved WRKY clade with specialized metabolic functions.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression in tobacco; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species differs from applied species.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression in tobacco alters accumulation of rutin, kaempferol, caffeic acid, and lignin
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtWRKY109669 altered rutin, kaempferol, caffeic acid, and lignin accumulation in tobacco. 2. It is discussed as an ortholog of AaWRKY1, suggesting conserved yet pathway-divergent functions.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Illustrates cross-species conservation and diversification of WRKY metabolic functions.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression in tobacco; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species differs from applied species.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY TF family; SECONDARY WALL THICKENING IN PITH
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Medicago STP regulates lignin biosynthesis. 2. It is orthologous to Arabidopsis WRKY12, illustrating conserved metabolic roles across species.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved regulator of lignification across species.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates capsidiol production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbWRKY8 regulates capsidiol production. 2. It is phosphorylated by the MPK3/MPK6 cascade. 3. It belongs to a conserved WRKY group independently involved in specialized metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: MAPK-responsive terpene regulator associated with conserved WRKY metabolic function.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates momilactone A metabolism and differentially modulates phenylpropanoid versus terpenoid gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY13 is reported to regulate momilactone A. 2. Overexpression up-regulates phenylpropanoid-pathway genes while down-regulating terpenoid biosynthetic genes. 3. It regulates over 500 genes including 39 TFs and binds promoters of other WRKY TFs, indicating a high regulatory hierarchy.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Top-tier rice WRKY that differentially redirects metabolic programs between phenylpropanoid and terpenoid pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior overexpression and transcriptomic study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: up-regulates phenylpropanoid genes; down-regulates terpenoid biosynthetic genes; binds other WRKY promoters
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for broad metabolic reprogramming, but pleiotropy should be considered.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May broadly alter multiple TFs and pathways due to extensive downstream network control.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positive regulator of momilactone, phytocassane, and oryzalexin accumulation by priming biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY45 positively regulates accumulation of momilactone, phytocassane, and oryzalexin. 2. It primes biosynthetic gene expression. 3. It is orthologous to CrWRKY1, indicating conserved specialized metabolism regulation across species. 4. It is also described as a MAPK cascade target in the review's future perspective section.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator of rice diterpene phytoalexin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: diterpene biosynthetic genes (not individually specified here)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for boosting rice defense diterpenes.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enhances momilactone production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY53 enhances momilactone production. 2. It is phosphorylated by the MPK3/MPK6 cascade. 3. It belongs to a conserved WRKY clade independently linked to specialized metabolism in multiple species.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive MAPK-responsive regulator of rice momilactone biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: repression of terpene and phenylpropanoid phytoalexin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses terpene synthesis and suppresses sakuranetin production while activating cold stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY76 represses terpene synthesis in rice. 2. It suppresses production of the phenylpropanoid sakuranetin. 3. It simultaneously activates cold stress tolerance. 4. It is pathogen responsive, indicating interconnected defense and metabolic signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator that redirects defense output away from specialized metabolite accumulation toward stress tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Magnaporthe oryzae-responsive context; cold stress context
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced cold tolerance accompanies reduced phytoalexin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important cautionary target because defense-metabolite repression may trade off with stress adaptation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates lignin
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY89 is reported to regulate lignin. 2. The review notes this case as illustrating how modulating specialized metabolism regulation may alter plant physiology and crop performance.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Example of a WRKY where metabolic regulation is linked to broader agronomic physiology.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Modulating this regulator may alter physiology and crop performance.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with increased ginsenoside production; ectopic expression in Arabidopsis up-regulates triterpene biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PqWRKY1 is associated with increased production of ginsenosides. 2. Heterologous expression in Arabidopsis up-regulates triterpene biosynthetic genes. 3. This demonstrates the usefulness of expression-metabolite association and heterologous expression to identify regulators in long-lived plants.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cross-species functional evidence supports PqWRKY1 as a triterpene pathway regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression-metabolite association; ectopic expression in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for ginsenoside engineering and for regulator discovery in medicinal plants.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species differs from applied species in the heterologous assay.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates expression of three monoterpene synthase genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlWRKY73 activates three monoterpene synthase genes. 2. This suggests that one WRKY can regulate multiple distinct biosynthetic pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator of multiple monoterpene synthase genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: fine-tunes the expression of a rate-limiting gene in paclitaxel biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcWRKY1 regulates paclitaxel biosynthesis. 2. It fine-tunes expression of a rate-limiting gene in the pathway. 3. Together with AaWRKY1, it supports the concept that WRKYs can control critical bottlenecks in specialized metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Controls a rate-limiting step in taxol biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: a rate-limiting paclitaxel pathway gene (not named in this review text)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for paclitaxel metabolic engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvWRKY2 regulates lignin biosynthesis. 2. The review notes this case as illustrating that modulating specialized metabolism may alter plant physiology and crop performance. 3. It belongs to a conserved WRKY ortholog group linked to specialized metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved phenylpropanoid regulator with agronomic consequences.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior functional study; review summary
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter plant physiology and crop performance.
